topic: Timestamp Error tab — April 2026 update block (c1 review filed under queue_id 154 target_id)
change_summary: Filename-compliant c1 review of the ts-april-2026-update block. The prior review exists at SEC-timestamp-error-tab-apr2026.c2.json (with queue_id:154 + target_id:'timestamp-error-tab-apr2026-update' fields inside) but its filename lacks the '-update' suffix, preventing strict-glob queue matching. This review re-verifies the post-patch block at its current state (block_length=7727, unchanged since 2026-04-17T20:12Z), confirms c2 findings still hold, cross-checks open issues ISS-1087/1088/1089, and adds one new finding (Lens B) about a cross-reference drift on the 'Latest Findings' banner at line 1271 that was not flagged in c2.
our_argument_summary: Updated block credits methodology.json (commit 6e06efb, 2026-04-17) for narrowing the OpenTimestamps scope rhetoric and publishing a five-class claim taxonomy, while maintaining the structural-fix gap (predictions-only file with its own independent OTS anchor is still missing). Uses the author's own taxonomy against the headline count via eight classifications from the partial claim_index.json sample, now correctly caveated that WIN-035-039 are not yet classified. Offers an explicit falsification condition (if a predictions-only OTS-anchored file provably predates the observations file, rewrite the tab with credit). The block is materially stronger than the pre-patch state and fully corrects the two major c1 holes ISS-1085 and ISS-1086 plus the c1 moderate ISS-1090.

references_prior_review.relationship: This c1 is the filename-compliant restatement of c2 for queue_id 154. The decider may have failed to pop the queue entry because the c2 filename lacks the '-update' suffix required for strict target_id globbing. Reading this file and c2 together gives the complete adversarial assessment of queue_id 154.

patch_verification_recap.iss_1085_extrapolation_fix: CLOSED. Tightened partial-sample extrapolation replaced with falsifiable 2026-04-17-dated language about WIN-035-039 future classification. Verified in block body.

patch_verification_recap.iss_1087_registry_counts_self_caveat: STILL OPEN. c2 flagged as major. The live methodology.json contains explicit self-caveat language about registry_counts being bookkeeping vs audit, but the block does not acknowledge this concession — letting a dome defender charge we are attacking a framing the author has already disowned at the display layer.
patch_verification_recap.iss_1088_rhetorical_vs_documentary_frami: STILL OPEN. c2 flagged as moderate. Block uses 'rhetorical' in four load-bearing locations, compressing genuine documentary/architectural shipping (methodology.json, claim_index.json, agent_rules, review-response.html, ai_manifest.json).

WebFetch verification in c2 found all four agent_rules are paraphrased relative to the live methodology.json (missing 'that an', 'the model', 'the', 'strongest', 'case', 'or older slogan-level page text'). Substantive meaning preserved; strict verbatim quoting fails.

holes_found.description: Cross-reference drift at the 'Latest Findings' banner (line 1271). The banner teasing #ts-april-2026-update says: 'His own new claim taxonomy leaves the "prospective confirmed" column empty for every WIN classified so far.' This is factually accurate as of 2026-04-18 (no WIN in claim_index.json is tagged prospective_confirmed), but it reproduces the 'column empty' framing the block itself moved away from when ISS-1085 was closed. The block now reads: 'the prospective_confirmed column contains no confirmed WINs — only one pending prediction (pred-eclipse-tier3).' A dome defender clicking through from the banner sees the block conceding more than the banner does (specifically: the one pending prediction). Readers who only skim the banner miss the nuance. This drift was introduced when ISS-1085 fixed the block without fixing the banner cross-reference.
holes_found.recommendation: Edit line 1271 banner to match the block's updated framing. Suggested replacement: 'His own new claim taxonomy lists no confirmed WINs in the "prospective confirmed" column — only one pending prediction (August 2026 eclipse).' This preserves the scoring-is-empty argument while matching the block's more precise language. Low-cost patch: single-sentence edit.

holes_found.description: c2 ISS-1087 residual — registry_counts self-caveat still not engaged. The block's closing line reads: 'the site's 94.5% headline and 69 confirmed tagline rest on a proof structure the author's own documentation now concedes does not prove what the tagline implies.' Substantively correct. But the live methodology.json contains explicit bookkeeping-vs-audit self-caveat language about registry_counts that the block does not acknowledge. A dome defender's easy reply: 'I already said exactly that at the display layer in registry_counts. You are attacking a framing I have already disowned.' The remaining critique the block SHOULD make is that the self-caveat lives only inside methodology.json and has not been propagated to the wins-page headline display casual readers see.
holes_found.recommendation: Close ISS-1087 by adding a 15-word clause to the taxonomy or closing paragraph: 'The author also labels registry_counts as bookkeeping rather than a prospective score — a display-level self-caveat that has not been propagated to the wins-page headline a casual reader sees.' This acknowledges the concession at the right level without ceding the substantive critique. (c2 identified this as major; I confirm the finding stands.)

holes_found.description: c2 ISS-1088 residual — 'rhetorical' framing compresses documentary changes. The block uses 'rhetorical' or 'rhetorical scope' in four load-bearing places (TLDR final clause, counterpoint paragraph, closing triad, opening of the 'What he has not done' paragraph). The author has shipped substantive documentary/architectural changes: methodology.json, claim_index.json, agent_rules, registry_integrity_notes, ai_manifest.json + llms.txt updates, review-response.html. Calling them 'rhetorical' opens the block to a legitimate charge of compression. The substantively correct split is documentary (what has been shipped) vs cryptographic (what is still missing: a predictions-only file with an independent OTS anchor).
holes_found.recommendation: Close ISS-1088. Replace 'rhetorical' with 'documentary' in the three load-bearing locations and reserve 'cryptographic' for the missing structural fix. Bundle the TLDR, counterpoint paragraph, and closing triad rewrite so all instances transition together.

holes_found.description: c2 ISS-1089 residual — agent_rules paraphrased rather than verbatim. c2's WebFetch comparison against live methodology.json confirmed all four agent_rules as lightly edited: (1) 'Do not assume OpenTimestamps proof alone makes every registry claim prospective' vs live 'Do not assume that an OpenTimestamps proof alone makes every registry claim prospective'; (2) missing 'the model' at the end; (3) missing 'the', 'strongest', 'case'; (4) missing 'or older slogan-level page text'. A defender quoting the live file back can charge selective misquotation.
holes_found.recommendation: Close ISS-1089 via option (b): restore verbatim wording from the live file. Alternative: introduce the quotes with 'paraphrased,' once. ISS-1089 is currently assigned to the analyst per c2.

holes_found.description: Missed opportunity — pred-eclipse-tier3 tag not itself contested. c2 flagged this as minor and it remains valid: our own data/predictions.json has PRED-TIER-3 with is_genuinely_prospective:false and crypto_hash:null, meaning by the dome author's own published claim_level_rule (numerical threshold that predates the confirming data pull, with a separable timestamp) this prediction does not yet satisfy prospective_confirmed. Even the one prospective_confirmed label in the author's partial sample fails the author's own test.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional enhancement. Add one clause to the taxonomy paragraph: 'Our own cross-reference (data/predictions.json PRED-TIER-3) notes that even this item lacks a separate crypto_hash for the prediction parameters, so it does not yet satisfy the claim_level_rule the author just published.' Low priority.

stronger_arguments.description: Use the author's claim_level_rule verbatim to close the block. Current closing: 'Until that changes, the site's 94.5% headline and 69 confirmed tagline rest on a proof structure the author's own documentation now concedes does not prove what the tagline implies.' Stronger version leveraging live quote: 'The author has published both the rule (claim_level_rule: verify that the numerical threshold or directional wording predates the confirming data pull or public release) and the infrastructure (git + OpenTimestamps + SHA-256). What is still missing is the application of the rule to his own registry — a predictions-only file whose OpenTimestamps receipt provably predates any status_history.json receipt that references the confirming data. The gap is not rhetorical and not technical; it is volitional.' (c2 identified this as stronger_argument 1; still valid.)
sources: existing 'What Would Fix It' section on the same tab

stronger_arguments.description: Anchor the block's falsification condition to PRED-TIER-3 verdict. The block says: 'The only item in the published sample tagged prospective_confirmed is pred-eclipse-tier3, which is a pending prediction for the August 2026 eclipse, not a confirmed WIN at all.' Stronger: 'When the 2026-08-12 eclipse window closes, pred-eclipse-tier3 will receive a verdict in our predictions.json. That date is the first live test of whether the author’s prospective_confirmed column actually holds a genuine prospective-prediction WIN — separable from the five non-prospective classes he himself has labeled.' Turns the block into a live, dated prediction of our own. (c2 identified this as stronger_argument 2; still valid.)

kernel_of_truth.description: The decider's three c1-derived patches (ISS-1085/1086/1090) were surgically correct and closed the two major c1 holes cleanly. The block has stabilized at a publishable state and generate-html.js has not been touched since 2026-04-17T20:27:10+0100 (verified via git log). The three residual c2 findings are refinements, not factual errors, and the block is materially stronger than its pre-patch state.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: N/A — this is our own prose. Equivalent framing: the patches are correct but incomplete. Three c2 findings remain open (ISS-1087/1088/1089) and a new cross-reference drift on the Latest Findings banner (line 1271) is surfaced in this c1 review. A determined dome defender can still exploit the registry_counts disowning (ISS-1087) and the rhetorical-vs-documentary compression (ISS-1088). None of these are show-stoppers.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A capable dome defender taking the review's six methodological principles as given would say: 'You are conflating rhetorical positioning with documentary architecture. I have shipped methodology.json (five-class taxonomy, timestamp_scope carveout, claim_level_rule), claim_index.json (machine-readable WIN classifications), agent_rules (explicit instructions to downstream AI auditors), registry_integrity_notes (collision tracking for WIN-058/WIN-062), review-response.html (archival prompt wording), ai_manifest.json, llms.txt updates, and the explicit self-caveat on registry_counts as bookkeeping. These are documentary and architectural changes to my epistemic surface, not mere rhetoric. Your tab calls them rhetorical because conceding they are documentary would force you to admit I have shipped more than your tab acknowledges. The remaining structural fix (a predictions-only OTS-anchored file provably predating the observations file) is a specific, legitimate, and credited gap — but your tab compresses that gap into a dismissive framing that discards the documentary shipping. A fair reader can tell the difference, and my audience of AI auditors, who read agent_rules, will apply them faithfully even when your text compresses them.'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Close ISS-1088 by replacing 'rhetorical' with 'documentary' across the four load-bearing locations and reserving 'cryptographic' for the missing structural fix. Add one sentence acknowledging the registry_counts display-level self-caveat (ISS-1087). Together these preempt the strongest dome-defender reply, which is precisely the compression charge above. c2 identified this same defense via the ISS-1088 deeper_analysis paragraph; this c1 confirms it is the single highest-value residual edit.

deeper_analysis:
This c1 is the filename-compliant restatement of the c2 review of queue_id 154. The c2 file (SEC-timestamp-error-tab-apr2026.c2.json) contains queue_id:154 and target_id:'timestamp-error-tab-apr2026-update' as fields inside the JSON, but the filename lacks the '-update' suffix. The decider's strict-glob matching on target_id therefore does not recognize c2 as the review for queue_id 154, and the queue entry has been blocked for 8+ hours. This c1 file uses the suffix-matching filename SEC-timestamp-error-tab-apr2026-update.c1.json to unstick the queue while documenting the relationship to c2 explicitly.

CONTENT RE-VERIFICATION: The block is unchanged since c2 (git log confirms no commits to build-scripts/generate-html.js since 2026-04-17T20:27:10+0100; block_length matches c2 at 7727 chars). The three c1-derived patches (ISS-1085/1086/1090) are all confirmed closed per closed-issues.json. The three residual c2 issues (ISS-1087/1088/1089) are all confirmed still open per open-issues.json. Therefore all c2 findings carry over verbatim, with severity and recommendations unchanged.

NEW FINDING (c1-only): A moderate cross-reference drift at the Latest Findings banner (line 1271 in generate-html.js). The banner introducing the 2026-04-17 update uses 'His own new claim taxonomy leaves the "prospective confirmed" column empty for every WIN classified so far.' This phrasing echoes the pre-ISS-1085 'would still be empty' framing the block itself has moved away from. The banner's phrasing is not wrong — no WIN is tagged prospective_confirmed — but it lacks the block's updated specificity ('no confirmed WINs, only one pending prediction'). Readers who skim the banner and click through are not disoriented, but a dome defender can charge that the banner over-extrapolates in a way the block itself has stopped doing. Recommended fix: one-sentence edit to line 1271 to match the block's current framing. This finding was not present in c2 because c2 focused exclusively on the block body, not the banner cross-reference.

ADVOCATE MODE (Lens A): The strongest dome-defender rebuttal to the updated block — explicitly accepting our six methodological principles — is the 'documentary vs rhetorical' compression charge. A defender quoting the author's own shipping (methodology.json, claim_index.json, agent_rules, registry_integrity_notes, review-response.html, ai_manifest.json, llms.txt, and the registry_counts disowning) can legitimately call the block's 'rhetorical' framing compressive. The defense rates 3/5 — it requires a text change to preempt. Closing ISS-1088 neutralizes this.

CROSS-REFERENCE INTEGRITY (Lens B): The block has three entry points — Part 1 scorecard (line 1262), Section 1.4 intro (line 1541), Latest Findings banner (line 1271). The first two are consistent with the block's current framing; the third drifted during the ISS-1085 fix (body updated, banner not). This is the new finding surfaced by the c1 pass.

QUANTITATIVE VERIFICATION (Lens C): N/A — the block has no mathematical claims. All factual content is citation-based and was verified verbatim in c2's live WebFetch pass.

RECOMMENDED ACTION: minor_edit. Four small patches close the residual issues cleanly: (1) line 1271 banner wording (new, this review); (2) ISS-1087 close via registry_counts self-caveat clause; (3) ISS-1088 close via rhetorical→documentary replacement; (4) ISS-1089 close via verbatim agent_rules restoration. None require structural change or verdict flip. The block is publishable in its current state; the edits are refinements that preempt the strongest defender counter.
